docs.title: PraisonAI Editor
docs.text:
AI-powered audio & video editor — transcribe, clean, and edit media with one command.

  • Transcribe

    Word-level timestamps in SRT, TXT, or JSON. OpenAI Whisper + local faster-whisper.

    transcribe

  • Edit

    Remove fillers, repetitions, silences, or keep only singing. One command.

    edit

  • Stem Separation

    Powered by Demucs — isolate vocals from instruments to find singing zones.

    demix

  • AI Agent

    "Remove the intro and any off-topic discussion about weather" — plain English.

    prompt edit

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    A[🎵 Input\nMP3 · MP4 · WAV] --> B[Probe\nmetadata]
    B --> C[Transcribe\nWhisper API]
    C --> D[Detect\nContent blocks]
    D --> E[Plan\nkeep / remove]
    E --> F[Render\nFFmpeg]
    F --> G[🎧 Output\nedited file]
    C -.->|cache| C
    D -.->|Demucs stems cache| D

docs.title: Install in 30 seconds
docs.text:
pip install praisonai-editor
export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...

praisonai-editor edit podcast.mp3 -v

docs.title: Feature overview
docs.text: Feature Command Extra install? Probe metadata probe No Convert format convert No Transcribe (Whisper) transcribe No Create edit plan plan No Edit (podcast/meeting/course…) edit No Content detection (ensemble) edit --detector ensemble No INA speech segmenter edit --detector ina [detect] Stem separation (Demucs) edit --demix [demix] Primary singing zone crop edit --demix --primary-zone [demix] AI agent editing edit --prompt "…" No Local Whisper edit --local [local]

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.10+
  • FFmpeg installed on your system
  • OpenAI API key (for cloud transcription, or use --local)

docs.title: Install FFmpeg
docs.text:
macOSUbuntu / DebianWindows 
brew install ffmpeg

sudo apt install ffmpeg

Download from ffmpeg.org and add to PATH.

docs.title: Minimal (OpenAI Whisper)
docs.text: pip install praisonai-editor

docs.title: With local Whisper (offline)
docs.text: pip install "praisonai-editor[local]"

docs.title: With Demucs stem separation
docs.text: pip install "praisonai-editor[demix]"

docs.title: With INA speech detector
docs.text: pip install "praisonai-editor[detect]"

docs.title: Everything
docs.text: pip install "praisonai-editor[all]"

docs.title: Extras at a glance
docs.text: Extra What it adds When to use (default) OpenAI Whisper cloud Always use first [local] faster-whisper (offline) No internet / privacy [demix] Demucs stem separation Music files, --demix [detect] inaSpeechSegmenter --detector ina [all] All of the above Power users

docs.title: Set your API key
docs.text:
export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...

Or create a .env file: 

OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...

No API key needed

Use --local flag to transcribe completely offline with faster-whisper: 

praisonai-editor edit file.mp3 --local

docs.title: Verify installation
docs.text:
praisonai-editor --help
praisonai-editor probe myfile.mp3

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text: Get from zero to an edited file in under 2 minutes.

docs.title: 1. Install
docs.text:
pip install praisonai-editor

export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...

Offline / local Whisper

pip install "praisonai-editor[local]"
praisonai-editor edit podcast.mp3 --local

docs.title: 2. Edit a podcast
docs.text:
praisonai-editor edit podcast.mp3 -v

What this does:

sequenceDiagram
    participant You
    participant CLI
    participant Whisper
    participant FFmpeg

    You->>CLI: edit podcast.mp3
    CLI->>Whisper: transcribe (word-level)
    Whisper-->>CLI: words + timestamps
    CLI->>CLI: detect fillers · repetitions · silences
    CLI->>FFmpeg: render keep-only segments
    FFmpeg-->>You: podcast_edited.mp3 ✓

docs.title: 3. Common recipes
docs.text:
Podcast cleanupKeep only singingMeeting — remove silences onlyAI natural language editTranscribe to SRTConvert MP4 → MP3 
praisonai-editor edit episode.mp3 --preset podcast -v

praisonai-editor edit concert.mp3 \
  --preset songs_only \
  --detector ensemble \
  --demix \
  --primary-zone \
  -v

praisonai-editor edit standup.mp4 --preset meeting -v

praisonai-editor edit interview.mp3 \
  --prompt "Remove the intro, the weather tangent, and keep only the technical discussion"

praisonai-editor transcribe video.mp4 --format srt --output subs.srt

praisonai-editor convert video.mp4 --format mp3

docs.title: 4. Output files
docs.text:
After edit, you get:

podcast_edited.mp3        ← edited audio
~/.praisonai/editor/podcast/
  ├── probe.json          ← media metadata
  ├── transcript.json     ← word timestamps (cached)
  ├── transcript.srt      ← subtitles
  ├── transcript.txt      ← plain text
  ├── plan.json           ← edit decisions
  └── content_blocks.json ← detection blocks (if --detector)

Transcript cache

Second run reuses transcript.json — no API call needed.

docs.title: AI Agent Prompt-Based Editing
docs.text: Edit media using plain English instructions. An AI agent interprets your prompt and creates a precise edit plan.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
praisonai-editor edit interview.mp3 \
  --prompt "Remove the intro, any weather discussion, and keep only the technical part"

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
    participant You
    participant CLI
    participant Whisper
    participant Agent
    participant FFmpeg

    You->>CLI: edit file.mp3 --prompt "..."
    CLI->>Whisper: transcribe (with cache)
    Whisper-->>CLI: transcript + timestamps
    CLI->>Agent: transcript + prompt → create edit plan
    Agent-->>CLI: JSON segments (keep/remove)
    CLI->>FFmpeg: render keep segments
    FFmpeg-->>You: edited file ✓

docs.title: Prompt examples
docs.text:
Remove introRemove off-topic sectionsKeep specific section 
praisonai-editor edit podcast.mp3 \
  --prompt "Remove the first 5 minutes of introduction"

praisonai-editor edit interview.mp3 \
  --prompt "Remove any discussion about weather or personal anecdotes, keep technical content only"

praisonai-editor edit lecture.mp3 \
  --prompt "Keep only the question and answer section at the end"

Fallback

If the agent cannot parse its own output, it falls back to the heuristic podcast preset automatically.

docs.title: Python API
docs.text:
from praisonai_editor.agent_pipeline import prompt_edit

result = prompt_edit(
    "interview.mp3",
    "Remove the intro and keep only technical discussion",
    output_path="interview_edited.mp3",
    verbose=True,
)

docs.title: Agent Tools
docs.text: Three built-in tools for use with PraisonAI agents. They auto-register via the praisonaiagents.tools entry-point.

docs.title: Available tools
docs.text: Tool name Class Description audio_editor AudioEditorTool Edit audio/video — remove fillers, silences, repetitions audio_transcribe AudioTranscribeTool Transcribe to text with word-level timestamps mp4_to_mp3 MP4ToMP3Tool Convert MP4 (or any format) to MP3

docs.title: Usage with an agent
docs.text:
from praisonaiagents import Agent
from praisonai_editor.agent_tool import (
    audio_editor_tool,
    audio_transcribe_tool,
    mp4_to_mp3_tool,
)

agent = Agent(
    instructions="You are a media processing assistant.",
    tools=[audio_editor_tool, audio_transcribe_tool, mp4_to_mp3_tool],
)

agent.start("Transcribe the interview and then clean it up for a podcast")

docs.title: Tool signatures
docs.text:
AudioEditorToolAudioTranscribeToolMP4ToMP3Tool 
audio_editor_tool.run(
    input_path="podcast.mp3",
    output_path="podcast_clean.mp3",  # optional
    preset="podcast",                  # podcast | meeting | course | clean
    use_local_whisper=False,
)

audio_transcribe_tool.run(
    audio_path="podcast.mp3",
    use_local_whisper=False,
    language="en",   # empty = auto-detect
)
# Returns: {"text": "...", "words": [...], "srt": "..."}

mp4_to_mp3_tool.run(
    input_path="video.mp4",
    output_path="audio.mp3",  # optional
    bitrate="192k",
)
# Returns: {"input_path": "...", "output_path": "..."}

docs.title: Python API Overview
docs.text: Use praisonai-editor directly from Python — no CLI needed.

docs.title: Core entry point
docs.text:
from praisonai_editor.pipeline import edit_media

result = edit_media("podcast.mp3", preset="podcast", verbose=True)
print(result.output_path)    # "podcast_edited.mp3"
print(result.success)        # True

docs.title: Pipeline flow
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    A["edit_media()"] --> B[auto-detect\naudio vs video]
    B -->|audio| C["edit_audio()"]
    B -->|video| D["edit_video()"]
    C --> E[probe → transcribe\n→ plan → render]
    D --> E

docs.title: Module layout
docs.text: Module Key exports pipeline edit_media, edit_audio, edit_video probe probe_media, FFmpegProber transcribe transcribe_audio, OpenAITranscriber, LocalTranscriber convert convert_media plan create_edit_plan, HeuristicEditor detect create_content_plan render FFmpegAudioRenderer, FFmpegVideoRenderer _demix isolate_vocals, has_demucs models EditPlan, EditResult, TranscriptResult, ProbeResult protocols Transcriber, Editor, Renderer, Prober, Converter

docs.title: edit_media() — Main API
docs.text:
from praisonai_editor.pipeline import edit_media

result = edit_media(input_path, output_path=None, **kwargs)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Default Description input_path str required Path to audio or video file output_path str auto Output path (default: {stem}_edited.{ext}) preset str "podcast" Edit preset detector str "auto" Content detector demix bool False Enable Demucs stem separation primary_zone_only bool False Crop to primary singing zone remove_fillers bool True Remove filler words remove_repetitions bool True Remove repeated words remove_silence bool True Remove long silences min_silence float 1.5 Minimum silence duration (seconds) use_local_whisper bool False Use faster-whisper (offline) language str None Language code (en, ta, es, …) copy_codec bool True Copy codec (faster vs re-encode) verbose bool False Print progress save_artifacts bool True Save transcript/plan/blocks to disk

docs.title: Returns: EditResult
docs.text:
result.success         # bool
result.output_path     # str
result.input_path      # str
result.probe           # ProbeResult
result.transcript      # TranscriptResult
result.plan            # EditPlan
result.error           # str | None
result.artifacts       # dict[str, str]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
SimpleMusic extractionCustom silence threshold 
from praisonai_editor.pipeline import edit_media

result = edit_media("podcast.mp3")

result = edit_media(
    "concert.mp3",
    preset="songs_only",
    detector="ensemble",
    demix=True,
    primary_zone_only=True,
    verbose=True,
)

result = edit_media(
    "recording.mp3",
    preset="podcast",
    min_silence=2.0,
    remove_fillers=False,
)

docs.title: Data Models
docs.text: All models live in praisonai_editor.models and are plain Python dataclasses.

docs.title: ProbeResult
docs.text:
from praisonai_editor.probe import probe_media

info = probe_media("video.mp4")
info.path               # str
info.duration           # float (seconds)
info.has_video          # bool
info.is_audio_only      # bool (property)
info.audio_codec        # "aac"
info.audio_sample_rate  # 48000
info.audio_channels     # 2
info.video_codec        # "h264"
info.width, info.height # 1920, 1080
info.fps                # 30.0
info.size_bytes         # int
info.to_dict()          # dict

docs.title: EditResult
docs.text:
result.success          # bool
result.input_path       # str
result.output_path      # str
result.probe            # ProbeResult | None
result.transcript       # TranscriptResult | None
result.plan             # EditPlan | None
result.error            # str | None
result.artifacts        # dict[str, str]  {name: path}
result.to_dict()        # dict

docs.title: Edit Plans API
docs.text: An EditPlan is a list of segments with keep or remove actions.

docs.title: create_edit_plan()
docs.text:
from praisonai_editor.plan import create_edit_plan

plan = create_edit_plan(
    transcript,
    duration=1823.4,
    preset="podcast",        # or specify kwargs:
    remove_fillers=True,
    remove_repetitions=True,
    remove_silence=True,
    min_silence=1.5,
)

docs.title: EditPlan
docs.text:
plan.segments            # list[Segment]
plan.original_duration   # float
plan.edited_duration     # float
plan.removed_duration    # float
plan.removal_summary     # {"filler": 12.3, "silence": 45.6, ...}

plan.get_keep_segments()   # list[Segment] with action="keep"
plan.get_remove_segments() # list[Segment] with action="remove"

docs.title: Segment
docs.text:
seg.start       # float (seconds)
seg.end         # float (seconds)
seg.action      # "keep" | "remove"
seg.reason      # "Filler word: 'um'"
seg.category    # "filler" | "repetition" | "silence" | "content"
seg.confidence  # 0.0–1.0

docs.title: Individual detectors
docs.text:
from praisonai_editor.plan import detect_fillers, detect_repetitions, detect_silences

fillers    = detect_fillers(transcript.words)
reps       = detect_repetitions(transcript.words, window=3)
silences   = detect_silences(transcript.words, duration, min_silence=1.5)

docs.title: Transcription API
docs.text:
from praisonai_editor.transcribe import transcribe_audio, OpenAITranscriber

result = transcribe_audio("podcast.mp3", language="en")

docs.title: transcribe_audio()
docs.text:
Convenience function. Automatically chooses OpenAI or local Whisper.

from praisonai_editor.transcribe import transcribe_audio

result = transcribe_audio(
    audio_path,
    use_local=False,  # True → faster-whisper
    language=None,    # None → auto-detect
)

docs.title: OpenAITranscriber
docs.text:
from praisonai_editor.transcribe import OpenAITranscriber

transcriber = OpenAITranscriber()
result = transcriber.transcribe("audio.mp3", language="en")
  • Automatically chunks audio > 10 minutes
  • Skips chunks < 5,000 bytes (near-empty last chunk)
  • 600-second timeout on the OpenAI client

docs.title: LocalTranscriber
docs.text:
from praisonai_editor.transcribe import LocalTranscriber

transcriber = LocalTranscriber()
result = transcriber.transcribe("audio.mp3")

Requires: pip install "praisonai-editor[local]" (faster-whisper)

docs.title: TranscriptResult
docs.text:
result.text            # full text string
result.words           # list[Word]
result.language        # "en"
result.duration        # float (seconds)

result.to_srt()        # SRT subtitle string
result.to_dict()       # dict (JSON-serializable)

docs.title: Word
docs.text:
word.text              # "Hello"
word.start             # 0.52  (seconds)
word.end               # 1.10  (seconds)
word.confidence        # 0.99

docs.title: Artifacts & Caching
docs.text: Every edit or transcribe run saves artifacts to ~/.praisonai/editor/{filename}/.

docs.title: What is saved
docs.text:
~/.praisonai/editor/{filename}/
  ├── probe.json           ← media metadata
  ├── transcript.json      ← word-level timestamps (CACHED)
  ├── transcript.srt       ← SRT subtitles
  ├── transcript.txt       ← plain text
  ├── plan.json            ← edit decisions (keep/remove segments)
  └── content_blocks.json  ← detection blocks (only with --detector)

~/.praisonai/editor/.demix_cache/{sha256}/
  ├── vocals.wav           ← separated vocals (CACHED)
  └── no_vocals.wav        ← instruments (CACHED)

docs.title: Caching behaviour
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
    participant CLI
    participant Cache
    participant API

    CLI->>Cache: Is transcript.json present?
    alt Cache hit
        Cache-->>CLI: ↻ Load transcript
    else Cache miss
        CLI->>API: Call Whisper API
        API-->>CLI: Transcript
        CLI->>Cache: Save transcript.json
    end

    CLI->>Cache: Is .demix_cache/{hash} present?
    alt Stem cache hit
        Cache-->>CLI: ↻ Reuse vocals.wav + no_vocals.wav
    else Cache miss
        CLI->>CLI: Run Demucs (~10 min)
        CLI->>Cache: Save stems
    end

docs.title: Disable artifact saving
docs.text: praisonai-editor edit podcast.mp3 --no-artifacts

docs.title: Clear caches
docs.text:
# Remove transcript cache for a specific file
rm ~/.praisonai/editor/podcast/transcript.json

# Remove all stem caches
rm -rf ~/.praisonai/editor/.demix_cache/

# Remove everything
rm -rf ~/.praisonai/editor/

docs.title: probe.json example
docs.text:
{
  "path": "podcast.mp3",
  "duration": 1823.45,
  "has_video": false,
  "audio_codec": "mp3",
  "audio_sample_rate": 44100,
  "audio_channels": 2,
  "size_bytes": 44352512
}

docs.title: plan.json example
docs.text:
{
  "original_duration": 1823.4,
  "edited_duration": 1680.2,
  "removed_duration": 143.2,
  "removal_summary": {"filler": 12.3, "silence": 122.8, "repetition": 8.1},
  "segments": [
    {"start": 0, "end": 5.2, "action": "keep", "category": "content"},
    {"start": 5.2, "end": 5.8, "action": "remove", "category": "filler", "reason": "Filler: 'um'"}
  ]
}

docs.title: content_blocks.json example
docs.text:
{
  "resolved": [
    {
      "start": 0.0, "end": 367.0,
      "type": "singing",
      "detector": "ensemble",
      "rms_db": -18.5,
      "confidence": 0.91
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: convert — Convert between formats
docs.text: Convert between audio and video formats using FFmpeg.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: praisonai-editor convert INPUT [OPTIONS]

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Short Default Description INPUT Source media file --output -o {stem}.{format} Output file path --format -f mp3 Target format: mp3, wav, m4a --bitrate -b 192k Audio bitrate

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
MP4 → MP3High qualityWAV outputCustom output path 
praisonai-editor convert video.mp4
# → video.mp3 (192k)

praisonai-editor convert video.mp4 --bitrate 320k

praisonai-editor convert podcast.mp3 --format wav

praisonai-editor convert video.mp4 --output ~/Desktop/audio.mp3

docs.title: Bitrate guide
docs.text:
Bitrate Use case 64k Voice / speech (small file) 128k Standard quality 192k Good quality (default) 320k High quality / music

Python API

from praisonai_editor.convert import convert_media

output = convert_media("video.mp4", "audio.mp3", bitrate="192k")
print(output)  # "audio.mp3"

docs.title: edit — Full editing pipeline
docs.text: The main command. Runs transcription → content detection → plan → render in one shot.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: praisonai-editor edit INPUT [OPTIONS]

docs.title: All Options
docs.text: Option Short Default Description INPUT Input media file (audio or video) --output -o {stem}_edited.{ext} Output file path --preset -p podcast Edit preset (see Presets) --prompt Natural language instruction (AI agent) --detector auto Content detector: auto · ensemble · ina · librosa · ffmpeg --demix off Enable Demucs vocal stem separation --primary-zone off Crop to primary singing zone only --no-fillers off Keep filler words (don't remove) --no-repetitions off Keep repetitions --no-silence off Keep silences --local off Use offline faster-whisper --language auto Language code (en, ta, es, …) --reencode off Re-encode instead of stream copy (slower, better quality) --no-artifacts off Don't save artifacts to ~/.praisonai/editor/ --verbose -v off Print step-by-step progress

docs.title: Pipeline overview
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    Input --> Probe
    Probe --> Cache{Transcript\ncached?}
    Cache -->|Yes| Plan
    Cache -->|No| Whisper --> Plan
    Plan --> Detect{Content\npreset?}
    Detect -->|songs_only\nspeech_only\nno_silence| ContentDetect
    Detect -->|podcast\nmeeting\ncourse\nclean| Heuristic
    ContentDetect -->|--demix| Demucs
    ContentDetect --> Render
    Demucs --> Render
    Heuristic --> Render
    Render --> Output

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
Podcast (default)Keep only songsSpeech only (remove music)Video meeting cleanupAI agent editTamil languageOffline 

praisonai-editor edit podcast.mp3 -v
Removes: fillers · repetitions · silences > 1.5s

praisonai-editor edit concert.mp3 \
  --preset songs_only \
  --detector ensemble \
  --demix \
  --primary-zone \
  -v

praisonai-editor edit radio.mp3 --preset speech_only --detector ensemble

praisonai-editor edit meeting.mp4 --preset meeting -v

praisonai-editor edit interview.mp3 \
  --prompt "Remove the intro and any off-topic discussion"

praisonai-editor edit audio.mp3 --language ta -v

pip install "praisonai-editor[local]"
praisonai-editor edit audio.mp3 --local -v

docs.title: Artifacts saved
docs.text:
~/.praisonai/editor/{filename}/
  ├── probe.json
  ├── transcript.json   ← cached — reused on second run
  ├── transcript.srt
  ├── transcript.txt
  ├── plan.json
  └── content_blocks.json  ← only with --detector

Use --no-artifacts to skip saving.

docs.title: Python API
docs.text:
from praisonai_editor.pipeline import edit_media

result = edit_media(
    "podcast.mp3",
    output_path="podcast_clean.mp3",
    preset="podcast",
    verbose=True,
)

print(result.success)          # True
print(result.output_path)      # "podcast_clean.mp3"
print(result.plan.removed_duration)  # seconds removed

docs.title: plan — Preview the edit plan
docs.text: See what would be removed before actually editing the file.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: praisonai-editor plan INPUT [OPTIONS]

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Short Default Description INPUT Media file to plan --output -o stdout Save plan as JSON --preset -p podcast Edit preset --local False Use local Whisper

docs.title: Examples
Plan structure
docs.text:
flowchart TD
    A[Transcript] --> B[Detect fillers]
    A --> C[Detect repetitions]
    A --> D[Detect silences]
    B --> E[Merge overlapping]
    C --> E
    D --> E
    E --> F[Build keep/remove segments]
    F --> G[EditPlan JSON]

No file is modified

plan only analyzes and outputs JSON — your file stays untouched.

docs.title: probe — Inspect media metadata
docs.text: Use probe to quickly check the metadata of any audio or video file.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: praisonai-editor probe INPUT [OPTIONS]

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Description INPUT Path to media file (MP3, MP4, WAV, …) --output FILE Save result as JSON file --json Print full JSON to stdout

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
BasicJSON outputSave to file 
praisonai-editor probe podcast.mp3

Output: 

File: podcast.mp3
Duration: 1823.45s
Type: Audio only
Audio codec: mp3
Audio: 44100Hz, 2ch
Size: 42.30 MB


praisonai-editor probe video.mp4 --json

{
  "path": "video.mp4",
  "duration": 3600.0,
  "has_video": true,
  "audio_codec": "aac",
  "audio_sample_rate": 48000,
  "audio_channels": 2,
  "video_codec": "h264",
  "width": 1920,
  "height": 1080,
  "fps": 30.0,
  "size_bytes": 524288000
}

praisonai-editor probe file.mp3 --output meta.json

docs.title: What is probe useful for?
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    A[media file] --> B[probe]
    B --> C{Audio or Video?}
    C -->|Audio only| D["Duration, codec,\nsample rate, channels"]
    C -->|Video| E["+ resolution, FPS,\nvideo codec"]

Python API

from praisonai_editor.probe import probe_media

info = probe_media("podcast.mp3")
print(info.duration)       # 1823.45
print(info.is_audio_only)  # True

docs.title: transcribe — Audio/video to text
docs.text: Transcribe any audio or video file with word-level timestamps.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: praisonai-editor transcribe INPUT [OPTIONS]

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Short Default Description INPUT Audio or video file --output -o stdout Output file path --format -f srt Output format: srt, txt, json --local False Use offline faster-whisper --language auto Language code e.g. en, ta, es

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
SRT subtitlesPlain textJSON with timestampsTamil (non-English)Offline 
praisonai-editor transcribe video.mp4 --format srt --output video.srt

1
00:00:00,000 --> 00:00:03,240
Welcome everyone to today's session.

2
00:00:03,800 --> 00:00:07,100
We'll be covering the main topics.

praisonai-editor transcribe podcast.mp3 --format txt

praisonai-editor transcribe audio.mp3 --format json --output words.json

{
  "text": "Welcome everyone to today's session.",
  "words": [
    {"text": "Welcome", "start": 0.0, "end": 0.52, "confidence": 0.99},
    {"text": "everyone", "start": 0.58, "end": 1.10, "confidence": 0.99}
  ],
  "language": "en",
  "duration": 1823.4
}

praisonai-editor transcribe audio.mp3 --language ta

pip install "praisonai-editor[local]"
praisonai-editor transcribe audio.mp3 --local

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    A[audio/video] --> B[Extract MP3]
    B --> C{File > 10 min?}
    C -->|Yes| D[Split into\n10-min chunks]
    C -->|No| E[Single API call]
    D --> F[Whisper API\n× N chunks]
    E --> F
    F --> G[Merge + offset\ntimestamps]
    G --> H[SRT / TXT / JSON]

Transcript cache

After transcribing, the result is cached at ~/.praisonai/editor/{file}/transcript.json. Running edit on the same file loads from cache — no repeat API calls.

Python API

from praisonai_editor.transcribe import transcribe_audio

result = transcribe_audio("podcast.mp3", language="en")
print(result.text)       # full text
print(result.to_srt())   # SRT subtitles
for w in result.words:
    print(w.start, w.text)

docs.title: 🎵 Stem Separation (Demix)
docs.text: Powered by Demucs — an AI model that separates audio into vocal and instrument tracks.

docs.title: Why stem separation?
docs.text:
Without demix, a segment where someone sings over a backing track is labeled speech_over_music — ambiguous.

With demix, that segment becomes precisely classified as singing — enabling clean extraction.

flowchart LR
    A[Concert recording] --> B{Without --demix}
    B --> C["speech_over_music 🤷"]
    A --> D{With --demix}
    D --> E["Demucs separates\nvocals + instruments"]
    E --> F["singing ✅"]
    E --> G["music ✅"]

docs.title: Install
docs.text: pip install "praisonai-editor[demix]"

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
praisonai-editor edit concert.mp3 \
  --preset songs_only \
  --detector ensemble \
  --demix \
  --primary-zone \
  -v

docs.title: What Demucs separates
docs.text: Stem File Description vocals vocals.wav Isolated vocal track no_vocals no_vocals.wav All instruments (no voice)

docs.title: Stem cache
docs.text:
The first run is slow (CPU inference on a 40-min file ≈ 10 min). After that, stems are cached forever:

~/.praisonai/editor/.demix_cache/{sha256_hash}/
  ├── vocals.wav       (first 8 MiB SHA-256 hash → unique per file)
  └── no_vocals.wav

Second run is ~8 seconds. See Stem Cache for details.

docs.title: Supported models
docs.text: Model Notes mdx_extra Default — best quality htdemucs More stems (drums, bass, etc.) mdx_extra_q Quantized — lower memory

docs.title: Python API
docs.text:
from praisonai_editor._demix import isolate_vocals, has_demucs

if has_demucs():
    vocals_path, inst_path = isolate_vocals(
        "concert.mp3",
        model_name="mdx_extra",  # default
        device="cpu",            # or "mps" for Apple Silicon
        verbose=True,
    )

docs.title: Stem Cache
docs.text: Demucs stem separation is slow on CPU (5–15 min for a 40-min file). The stem cache ensures it only runs once per file.

docs.title: How the cache key works
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    A[Input file] --> B[Read first 8 MiB]
    B --> C[SHA-256 hash]
    C --> D[First 16 hex chars]
    D --> E["~/.praisonai/editor/.demix_cache/{hash}/"]
    E --> F{vocals.wav + no_vocals.wav\nboth exist?}
    F -->|Yes| G[✅ Reuse — skip Demucs]
    F -->|No| H[Run Demucs → save]

docs.title: Cache location
docs.text:
~/.praisonai/editor/.demix_cache/
  └── a78a770eb2572ef6/
        ├── vocals.wav      (424 MB)
        └── no_vocals.wav   (424 MB)

docs.title: Timing example
docs.text: Run Time First run (40-min file, CPU) ~10 min Second run (cache hit) ~8 sec

docs.title: Clear the cache
docs.text: rm -rf ~/.praisonai/editor/.demix_cache/

docs.title: Python check
docs.text:
from praisonai_editor._demix import has_demucs, isolate_vocals
print(has_demucs())  # True / False

docs.title: Primary Zone Detection (--primary-zone)
docs.text: Automatically finds and extracts the main singing section from a long recording.

docs.title: What is a singing zone?
docs.text: A singing zone is a contiguous block where singing segments are close together. The primary zone is the longest/most significant zone — usually the main performance.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
A 40-min live recording might contain:

00:00 – 05:20  🗣️ Talking / intro
05:20 – 27:13  🎵 Main singing performance  ← PRIMARY ZONE
27:13 – 30:00  🗣️ Talking / outro
30:00 – 35:00  🎵 Short bonus songs

--primary-zone crops the output to 05:20–27:13 automatically.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
praisonai-editor edit concert.mp3 \
  --preset songs_only \
  --detector ensemble \
  --demix \
  --primary-zone \
  -v

Requires --demix

Primary zone detection works best with Demucs stem separation, which precisely identifies singing vs music.

docs.title: How the algorithm works
docs.text:
flowchart TD
    A[Classified blocks] --> B[Find all singing segments]
    B --> C[Merge singing segments\nwithin 30s of each other]
    C --> D[Score each zone\nby total singing duration]
    D --> E[Select highest-scoring zone]
    E --> F[Trim output to zone boundaries]

docs.title: Python API
docs.text:
result = edit_media(
    "concert.mp3",
    preset="songs_only",
    detector="ensemble",
    demix=True,
    primary_zone_only=True,
)

docs.title: Content Detection Overview
docs.text: Content detection classifies each second of your media into types like speech, music, singing, silence — enabling smart content-based editing.

docs.title: When is detection used?
docs.text:
Detection is required for these presets:

Preset What it removes songs_only Keeps music + singing speech_only Keeps speech no_silence Removes silence only

docs.title: Available detectors
docs.text:
flowchart TD
    A[--detector] --> B{Which?}
    B -->|auto / ensemble| C[🏆 Ensemble\nbest accuracy]
    B -->|ina| D[INA CNN\ngood for speech/music]
    B -->|librosa| E[Librosa spectral\nfeature-based]
    B -->|ffmpeg| F[FFmpeg astats\nfast heuristic]
    C --> G[Combine all available\ndetectors + vote]

docs.title: Detector comparison
docs.text: Detector Speed Accuracy Extra install? ensemble ⭐⭐⭐ ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ No ina ⭐⭐ ⭐⭐⭐⭐ [detect] librosa ⭐⭐⭐ ⭐⭐⭐ No ffmpeg ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ ⭐⭐ No

docs.title: Content block types
docs.text: Type Description speech Clear speech, no music music Music without vocals singing Vocal melody / song speech_over_music Talking over music bed talking_over_music Speech detected on music (with demix) silence No significant audio

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: praisonai-editor edit file.mp3 --preset songs_only --detector ensemble

docs.title: Python API
docs.text:
from praisonai_editor.detect import create_content_plan

plan, blocks, all_events = create_content_plan(
    "file.mp3", transcript, duration,
    keep_types=["music", "singing"],
    detector="ensemble",
    verbose=True,
)

docs.title: Ensemble detector (--detector ensemble)
docs.text: The recommended detector. Combines multiple detectors and uses a voting algorithm to pick the most confident classification for each segment.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
praisonai-editor edit file.mp3 --preset songs_only --detector ensemble

The --detector auto option also routes to ensemble.

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    A[Audio] --> B[FFmpeg astats]
    A --> C[Librosa spectral]
    A --> D[INA CNN\nif installed]
    A --> E[Demucs stems\nif --demix]
    B --> F[Ensemble decision\nconflict resolution]
    C --> F
    D --> F
    E --> F
    F --> G[Classified blocks\nwith confidence]

Conflict resolution rules: - If INA is available → INA has highest priority for speech/music boundaries - If Demucs is used → demix scores refine singing vs talking-over-music - FFmpeg provides base signal statistics for all segments

docs.title: Confidence scores
docs.text: Each block gets a confidence score (0–1). Low-confidence borders are re-classified using weighted voting from all available detectors.

docs.title: FFmpeg heuristic detector (--detector ffmpeg)
docs.text: Fastest detector. Uses FFmpeg's astats filter to get RMS/peak energy statistics per segment.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: praisonai-editor edit file.mp3 --preset no_silence --detector ffmpeg

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    A[Audio] --> B[ffmpeg astats]
    B --> C[RMS dB per 0.5s window]
    C --> D{Below silence\nthreshold?}
    D -->|Yes| E[silence]
    D -->|No| F[speech or music\nbased on energy]

docs.title: When to use ffmpeg
docs.text:
• Very long files where speed matters
  • Simple silence removal only (--preset no_silence)
  • When you don't need precise speech/music classification
  • Final step in a pipeline after rough cutting

docs.title: Limitations
docs.text:
• Cannot distinguish speech from music
  • No machine learning — accuracy is heuristic only

docs.title: INA Speech Segmenter (--detector ina)
docs.text: Uses a CNN (Convolutional Neural Network) trained on broadcast media to detect speech vs music vs noise vs silence.

docs.title: Install
docs.text: pip install "praisonai-editor[detect]"

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: praisonai-editor edit file.mp3 --preset speech_only --detector ina

docs.title: Strengths
docs.text:
• High accuracy for speech vs music boundary detection
  • Handles noisy environments well (broadcast quality)
  • Good at detecting short speech segments within music

docs.title: Weaknesses
docs.text:
• Slower (CNN inference)
  • Cannot distinguish singing from music (both labeled as music)
  • Use with --demix to get singing classification

docs.title: Content types returned
docs.text: INA label Mapped to speech speech music music noEnergy silence

docs.title: Librosa spectral detector (--detector librosa)
docs.text: Uses spectral features (RMS energy, spectral centroid, zero-crossing rate) from librosa to classify segments.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: praisonai-editor edit file.mp3 --preset songs_only --detector librosa

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    A[Audio frame] --> B[RMS energy dB]
    A --> C[Spectral centroid]
    A --> D[Zero-crossing rate]
    B --> E[Classify frame]
    C --> E
    D --> E
    E --> F{speech, music,\nsilence, singing?}

docs.title: Strengths
docs.text:
• No extra install (librosa is a base dependency after install)
  • Fast — runs on CPU without ML inference
  • Works fine for clean recordings

docs.title: Weaknesses
docs.text:
• Less accurate than ensemble or INA on noisy or complex audio
  • May confuse speech over music as pure music

docs.title: Presets Overview
docs.text:
Presets are named editing profiles. Pick the one closest to your use case.

flowchart TD
    A[Your file] --> B{What kind\nof content?}
    B -->|🎙️ Podcast recording| C[podcast]
    B -->|📋 Meeting recording| D[meeting]
    B -->|🎓 Course/lecture| E[course]
    B -->|🧹 Maximum cleanup| F[clean]
    B -->|🎵 Music - keep songs| G[songs_only]
    B -->|🗣️ Music - keep speech| H[speech_only]
    B -->|⏸️ Remove pauses only| I[no_silence]

docs.title: Preset comparison
docs.text: Preset Fillers Repetitions Silence Min gap Needs detector? podcast ✅ removed ✅ removed ✅ removed 1.5s No meeting ✅ removed ❌ kept ✅ removed 2.0s No course ✅ removed ✅ removed ✅ removed 1.0s No clean ✅ removed ✅ removed ✅ removed 0.8s No songs_only — — — — Yes speech_only — — — — Yes no_silence — — ✅ removed — Yes

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
praisonai-editor edit FILE --preset PRESET

Content presets also need --detector: 

praisonai-editor edit FILE --preset songs_only --detector ensemble

docs.title: clean preset
docs.text: Maximum cleanup. Tightest silence removal.

docs.title: What it removes
docs.text:
• Filler words
  • Repetitions
  • Silences > 0.8 seconds — very tight, produces a fast-paced output

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
praisonai-editor edit raw.mp3 --preset clean -v

Aggressive

0.8s silence threshold is aggressive. Rapid-fire speech may feel unnatural. Use course (1.0s) or podcast (1.5s) if results feel rushed.

docs.title: course preset
docs.text: Best for: online courses, lectures, tutorials, screencasts.

docs.title: What it removes
docs.text:
• Filler words (strict)
  • Repetitions (learners need clean delivery)
  • Silences > 1.0 second (tighter pacing for learning)

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: praisonai-editor edit lecture.mp4 --preset course -v

docs.title: Python API
docs.text: result = edit_media("lecture.mp4", preset="course")

docs.title: meeting preset
docs.text: Best for: team meetings, standups, recorded calls.

docs.title: What it removes
docs.text:
• Filler words: yes, but keeps interruptions and acknowledgements intact
  • Silences: gaps longer than 2.0 seconds (more lenient — people think a bit more in meetings)
  • Keeps repetitions — "Let me re-state that" is common and informative in meetings

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: praisonai-editor edit standup.mp4 --preset meeting -v

docs.title: Python API
docs.text: result = edit_media("meeting.mp4", preset="meeting")

docs.title: no_silence preset
docs.text: Keep everything — just remove silent gaps between segments.

docs.title: What it keeps
docs.text:
• All content: speech, music, singing, talking over music
  • Removes only silence blocks with no audio activity

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
praisonai-editor edit recording.mp3 \
  --preset no_silence \
  --detector ensemble \
  -v

docs.title: When to use
docs.text:
• Remove dead air from a recording without changing content type
  • Live recordings with gaps between songs or speakers
  • Tighten up any recording without changing what's said or played

docs.title: Python API
docs.text:
result = edit_media(
    "recording.mp3",
    preset="no_silence",
    detector="ensemble",
)

docs.title: podcast preset
docs.text: Best for: podcast recordings, interviews, solo recordings.

docs.title: What it removes
docs.text:
• Filler words: um, uh, er, ah, like, you know, basically, actually, literally…
  • Repetitions: same word repeated within 3 words
  • Silences: gaps longer than 1.5 seconds

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: praisonai-editor edit episode.mp3 --preset podcast -v

docs.title: Typical result
docs.text: Before After Saved 45 min ~38 min ~15%

docs.title: Python API
docs.text:
from praisonai_editor.pipeline import edit_media

result = edit_media("episode.mp3", preset="podcast")
print(f"Saved {result.plan.removed_duration:.0f}s")

docs.title: songs_only preset
docs.text: Extract only the singing/music portions from a recording. Removes speech, talking, and silence.

docs.title: What it keeps
docs.text:
• Music segments (instrumental sections)
  • Singing segments (vocal melody)
  • With --demix: only pure singing (vocals separated from instruments)

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
# Basic
praisonai-editor edit concert.mp3 \
  --preset songs_only \
  --detector ensemble \
  -v

# Best quality — with Demucs stem separation
praisonai-editor edit concert.mp3 \
  --preset songs_only \
  --detector ensemble \
  --demix \
  --primary-zone \
  -v

docs.title: How content detection works
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    A[Input audio] --> B[FFmpeg stats\ndetector]
    A --> C[Demucs stem\nseparation]
    B --> D[Ensemble\ndecision]
    C --> D
    D --> E{Block type?}
    E -->|music| F[✅ Keep]
    E -->|singing| F
    E -->|speech| G[❌ Remove]
    E -->|silence| G
    F --> H[Render output]
    G --> H

docs.title: With --demix and --primary-zone
docs.text:
flowchart TD
    A[Input] --> B[Demucs separates\nvocals + instruments]
    B --> C[Classify each segment]
    C --> D[Find singing zones]
    D --> E[Identify PRIMARY zone\nlargest singing block]
    E --> F[Trim to primary zone]
    F --> G[Output: just the concert]

Install Demucs

pip install "praisonai-editor[demix]"

docs.title: Python API
docs.text:
from praisonai_editor.pipeline import edit_media

result = edit_media(
    "concert.mp3",
    preset="songs_only",
    detector="ensemble",
    demix=True,
    primary_zone_only=True,
    verbose=True,
)

docs.title: speech_only preset
docs.text: Extract only speech portions — removes music, singing, and silence.

docs.title: What it keeps
docs.text:
• Speech segments (clear talking without music)
  • Talking over music (speech overlaid on music)
  • Speech over music (general speech+music mix)

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
praisonai-editor edit radio_show.mp3 \
  --preset speech_only \
  --detector ensemble \
  -v

docs.title: When to use
docs.text:
• Radio shows where you want interview clips without music beds
  • Live stream recordings — keep only the talking parts
  • Music shows — extract the host commentary only

Note

Requires --detector (a content detector). Use ensemble for best results.

docs.title: Python API
docs.text:
from praisonai_editor.pipeline import edit_media

result = edit_media(
    "radio.mp3",
    preset="speech_only",
    detector="ensemble",
    verbose=True,
)

docs.title: Extending with Protocols
docs.text: Every stage of the pipeline is defined as a Python Protocol — swap in your own implementation without touching core code.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
classDiagram
    class Prober {
        +probe(path: str) ProbeResult
    }
    class Converter {
        +convert(input, output, bitrate) str
    }
    class Transcriber {
        +transcribe(audio_path, language) TranscriptResult
    }
    class Editor {
        +create_plan(transcript, duration ...) EditPlan
    }
    class Renderer {
        +render(input, output, plan, copy_codec) str
    }

    class FFmpegProber { implements Prober }
    class FFmpegConverter { implements Converter }
    class OpenAITranscriber { implements Transcriber }
    class LocalTranscriber { implements Transcriber }
    class HeuristicEditor { implements Editor }
    class FFmpegAudioRenderer { implements Renderer }
    class FFmpegVideoRenderer { implements Renderer }

docs.title: Custom implementations
docs.text:
Pick the protocol you want to replace:

  • Custom Transcriber — use your own speech-to-text engine
  • Custom Editor — write your own editing logic
  • Custom Renderer — use your own rendering engine

docs.title: Custom Editor
docs.text: Write your own logic for creating edit plans (what to keep and remove).

docs.title: Protocol
docs.text:
from praisonai_editor.protocols import Editor
from praisonai_editor.models import EditPlan, TranscriptResult

class MyEditor:
    def create_plan(
        self,
        transcript: TranscriptResult,
        duration: float,
        *,
        remove_fillers: bool = True,
        remove_repetitions: bool = True,
        remove_silence: bool = True,
        min_silence: float = 1.5,
    ) -> EditPlan:
        ...

docs.title: Example: Remove only first N seconds
docs.text:
from praisonai_editor.models import EditPlan, Segment

class TrimIntroEditor:
    def __init__(self, intro_secs: float = 30.0):
        self.intro_secs = intro_secs

    def create_plan(self, transcript, duration, **kwargs):
        return EditPlan(
            segments=[
                Segment(0, self.intro_secs, "remove", "intro", "content"),
                Segment(self.intro_secs, duration, "keep", "main content", "content"),
            ],
            original_duration=duration,
            edited_duration=duration - self.intro_secs,
            removed_duration=self.intro_secs,
        )

docs.title: Custom Renderer
docs.text: Replace FFmpeg rendering with your own engine (e.g., moviepy, pydub).

docs.title: Protocol
docs.text:
from praisonai_editor.protocols import Renderer
from praisonai_editor.models import EditPlan

class MyRenderer:
    def render(
        self,
        input_path: str,
        output_path: str,
        plan: EditPlan,
        *,
        copy_codec: bool = True,
    ) -> str:
        ...

docs.title: Example: pydub renderer
docs.text:
from pydub import AudioSegment
from praisonai_editor.models import EditPlan

class PydubRenderer:
    def render(self, input_path, output_path, plan, *, copy_codec=True):
        audio = AudioSegment.from_file(input_path)
        result = AudioSegment.empty()
        for seg in plan.get_keep_segments():
            result += audio[seg.start * 1000 : seg.end * 1000]
        result.export(output_path, format="mp3")
        return output_path

docs.title: Custom Transcriber
docs.text: Replace OpenAI Whisper with any speech-to-text engine.

docs.title: Protocol
docs.text:
from praisonai_editor.protocols import Transcriber
from praisonai_editor.models import TranscriptResult, Word

class MyTranscriber:
    def transcribe(
        self,
        audio_path: str,
        *,
        language: str | None = None,
    ) -> TranscriptResult:
        ...

docs.title: Example: AssemblyAI
docs.text:
import assemblyai as aai
from praisonai_editor.models import TranscriptResult, Word

class AssemblyAITranscriber:
    def transcribe(self, audio_path, *, language=None):
        transcriber = aai.Transcriber()
        t = transcriber.transcribe(audio_path)
        words = [
            Word(text=w.text, start=w.start/1000, end=w.end/1000)
            for w in t.words
        ]
        return TranscriptResult(text=t.text, words=words, language=language or "en")

# Use it in the pipeline manually
from praisonai_editor.probe import FFmpegProber
from praisonai_editor.plan import create_edit_plan
from praisonai_editor.render import FFmpegAudioRenderer

prober = FFmpegProber()
probe = prober.probe("podcast.mp3")

transcriber = AssemblyAITranscriber()
transcript = transcriber.transcribe("podcast.mp3")

plan = create_edit_plan(transcript, probe.duration, preset="podcast")

renderer = FFmpegAudioRenderer()
renderer.render("podcast.mp3", "podcast_edited.mp3", plan)
